key_points: Double rooms are notably smaller than peer schools' doubles
Centennial Hall reportedly fits three students in one room
Campus is vertical and spread out; cold weather makes dorm location matter
key_quotes: Many of KSU's dorm rooms are too small, especially the 'doubles'... almost twice the size of what KSU offers at my out-of-state school in the 80s
Three in a room at Centennials
When it is cold outside, it isn't good to walk to class

key_points: Greek life (Alpha Phi) is active and well-documented by students
Making friends is harder without a roommate per student accounts
Described as a viable 'typical American college experience' school by exchange students
key_quotes: Hard to make friends if you don't have a roommate
Geared for kids who are very independent
Those are all good generic American college experience schools

key_points: No direct student complaints or praise about dining in the data
No specific dining hall mentions in any Reddit or YouTube source
Absence of dining commentary is itself notable for a 30,000-student school

key_points: Licensed psychologist salary posted at $53,217 for 30,000 students—a red flag for resources
Students openly discuss anxiety and mental health struggles in Kent-related posts
No mention of robust counseling services or wait times in any student post
key_quotes: Saw a post today for a licensed Psychologist at Kent State University... Salary is $53,217. Is this normal?
Just thinking about it is almost giving me an anxiety attack
Do my friends like me thoughts... our heads are just in overdrive

key_points: Generous merit scholarships available for above-average stats (32 ACT = ~$9,000)
Strong value for Ohio pre-med students avoiding debt before med school
International students receiving $7,000–$17,000 scholarships, making it competitive on cost
key_quotes: Kent, you don't want to be going into heavy debt before med school
Based on the chart it looks like you would get $9,000 in merit aid with your current stats
Kent gave me 7K scholarship... TOTAL- $34,084

key_points: Nursing program ranks higher than comparable regional schools like Truman State
Fashion and design programs are well-regarded; Kent-NEOMED pipeline is a real pre-med asset
One student 'absolutely hated' Kent at first, calmed down by year's end—adjustment curve is real
key_quotes: Kent works with NEOMED which is the plus for them
At the start of the year in Kent I absolutely hated the school, and nearly every aspect of it
Based on my google search, Kent State ranks higher for their nursing program

key_points: Rolling admissions and deferral policies are flexible per alumni accounts
Kent State sends an excessive volume of marketing emails—60+ reported by one student
Freshman course on 1970 shootings is required, showing some institutional self-awareness
key_quotes: I have fucking 60 emails from Kent State University
Kent State alum - you can defer if you want to... you can defer for up to a year
My freshmen year we were required to take a course that covered this tragedy

key_points: Campus is hilly and takes 20–30 minutes to walk end to end
Town of Kent is small and not as urban as it appears—limited off-campus options
Cold Ohio winters make the sprawling, vertical campus genuinely difficult to navigate
key_quotes: This college campus is mostly vertical. It takes about 20-30 minutes to walk from one end to the other
The town is small. It appears to be in a metropolitan area, but it isn't
When it is cold outside, it isn't good to walk to class

key_points: Architecture program flagged as high-unemployment (13.9%) by commenters
CS and cybersecurity programs seen as solid but not distinguished nationally
Fashion merchandising and design have name recognition; animation outcomes unclear
key_quotes: Architecture has one of the highest unemployment rates of any major at 13.9%
Kent is a solid school... it ranks better in your major [cybersecurity]
Kent state is not the kind of school that typically attracts out of state students. It isn't that good of a school

key_points: Outstanding in-state value, especially for pre-med students avoiding pre-med debt
Out-of-state cost draws skepticism—multiple commenters say it's not worth it
Merit scholarships can significantly offset costs for above-average Ohio students
key_quotes: Kent state is not the kind of school that typically attracts out of state students. It isn't that good of a school, and certainly not better than in-state options
It absolutely makes no sense to spend a significant amount of money on out-of-state tuition
Kent, you don't want to be going into heavy debt before med school

key_points: May 4, 1970 massacre defines the campus identity—memorials are active and emotional
Social life skews Greek, independent, and requires self-motivation to build community
Students describe gradual adjustment—initial dislike giving way to appreciation
key_quotes: Kent is an amazing atmosphere and one of the safest cities I have ever lived in

**Dorm Size:** Double rooms described as significantly smaller than comparable universities
**Reputation:** Multiple advisors explicitly say Kent is 'not worth out-of-state tuition' and 'not that good'

key_points: NEOMED partnership gives pre-med students a real accelerated medical school pipeline
Honors College offers priority course registration, research access, and boosted merit aid
Fashion design and merchandising program has genuine national recognition
